key_insight: Replacing the DFTB pairwise repulsive term with a many-body MACE correction creates a DFTB+MACE model that improves accuracy while preserving an explicit electronic structure description.
engineering_relevance: The framework enables larger-scale and longer-time simulations of magnesium compounds, including adsorption, molecular dynamics, and phonons, with lower cost than first-principles methods.
method: Train MACE on DFT–DFTB energy/force residuals, excluding the pair-potential contribution.
Embed the learned many-body correction into a DFTB model for magnesium-based systems.
Validate on MgO–CO2 adsorption, water clusters, and fcc/bcc MgO phonons versus DFT and pure MACE.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports improved agreement with DFT compared with DFTB plus a pair potential, with only a moderate computational-cost increase across multiple test cases.
watch_item: The original MACE training set may not cover the full potential-energy surface encountered in simulations, so broader data coverage may be needed for robust transferability.
paper_title: A Combined Tight Binding with Machine Learning Potential Model for Magnesium Compounds
